name: Medium Brand Buyer
description: Moderate purchaser who buys the brand 1-2 times per month
context: Medium Brand Buyer purchases this brand regularly but not obsessively—typically 1-2 times per month. They're familiar with the brand but also buy competitors. They see this brand as a solid option but not their only choice. They're receptive to marketing and promotions, and their purchase frequency can be increased with the right incentives. They represent the largest, most moveable segment.

psychographics.decisionMakingStyle: Flexible and promotional-responsive. Open to persuasion. Balances habit with exploration.

alwaysAsks: Why should I choose this over my other options?
Is this worth buying more often?
Will I remember this next time?
Does this fit my current mood?
evaluationCriteria.reactsToEarlyIdeas: Evaluates as a comparison shopper. Needs clear differentiation and compelling reasons to increase frequency.
evaluationCriteria.scoringRubric: Scores on distinctiveness, promotional appeal, memorability, mood fit, and reasons to buy more often.

exampleQuotes: I buy it sometimes, depends what I'm in the mood for
I like it, but I also like [competitor]
If it's on sale, I'll definitely grab it
I don't really think about brands that much
Sure, I'll try the new flavor
keyInsights: Medium buyers are the most moveable segment—can go up or down in frequency
They respond well to promotions and limited editions
They need reminders and reasons to choose you over competitors
Building mental availability is critical with this group
They're not loyal but they're not hostile either—just flexible
